items.title: Your annual reminder that agents are the people leaking contract extensions and signings, therefore we are getting the most player friendly version of the deal.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Your annual reminder that agents are the people leaking contract extensions and signings, therefore we are getting the most player friendly version of the deal. 

What’s often left out is the team friendly aspects of deals like non guaranteed money. We don’t always get the full context of a deal.

items.title: Quick thoughts on the DWade signing:
items.author: Derek Bodner
items.body_excerpt:
Quick thoughts on the DWade signing:
- Sixers really needed some defensive versatility at that spot.
- Solid floor spacer, albeit would love more volume / quicker release.
- Plays pretty much mistake-free basketball (5.1% turnover rate!), which will be a nice change of pace.

items.title: Okay just got to the bottom of this -- Keon Ellis's deal is a true mutual option for year 2. He's getting two years and $18 million unless both sides simultaneously opt out of it next June, which presumably would only happen if they had already agreed on his next contract. (1/2)
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt: Okay just got to the bottom of this -- Keon Ellis's deal is a true mutual option for year 2. items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Dean Wade will go into the Sixers non-taxpayer mid-level exception. They're now $16.6 million below the first apron hard cap.

They have about $6 million remaining of their MLE, but using it would push them up against the luxury tax line.

items.title: Keon Ellis has agreed to a 2-year, $18 million deal with the Brooklyn Nets, league sources confirmed to @fredkatz.bsky.social and me. The contract has a team AND player option for Year 2, so either side can choose to opt in.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Keon Ellis has agreed to a 2-year, $18 million deal with the Brooklyn Nets, league sources confirmed to @fredkatz.bsky.social and me. The contract has a team AND player option for Year 2, so either side can choose to opt in. 
Shams first on it.

Here is a flow chart for the deal:

items.title: It's likely that Collins' extension with the Bulls will include no more than a 5% raise from Year 1 to Year 2. That will make Collins immediately trade-eligible instead of triggering a six-month restriction.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
It's likely that Collins' extension with the Bulls will include no more than a 5% raise from Year 1 to Year 2. That will make Collins immediately trade-eligible instead of triggering a six-month restriction.

I would not expect an immediate trade, however. No sense that's the plan.

items.title: Pair of relevant notes on Kristaps Porzingis' new contract, per sources.
items.author: Anthony Slater
items.body_excerpt:
Pair of relevant notes on Kristaps Porzingis' new contract, per sources.

1. Only $3 million is guaranteed for the second season.

2. He's immediately trade eligible because of the structure of the two-year, $40 million extension (no more than 5% raise between yrs 1 and 2).

items.title: Because Zach Collins is signing an extension with the Chicago Bulls, it will eat into their cap space. (No timing this out to come after cap space is used.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Because Zach Collins is signing an extension with the Chicago Bulls, it will eat into their cap space. (No timing this out to come after cap space is used.

My new projected remaining cap space for Chicago is $22.6M with one standard roster spot open.

items.title: Hinkle Fieldhouse lands 2026 NBA Cup championship game
items.author: Fieldhouse Files
items.body_excerpt: Hinkle Fieldhouse has hosted basketball games at all levels. Hinkle Fieldhouse has hosted high school and college championship games, so why not add the NBA to the mix? Now it will. The historic gym, on the campus of Butler University, will host the Emirates NBA Cup 2026…
